criteria.criterion: `make worktree` with NO `name=` prints a usage message and exits non-zero.
`make worktree name=<tmp>` creates a worktree at `../<tmp>` on a new branch `<tmp>` whose HEAD equals current `origin/main`, containing a populated `.venv` with a working `rebar` on its path.
The target is listed by `make help` (has a `## ` description) and is in `.PHONY`.
`docs/local-dev-env.md` references `make worktree name=` as the one-command setup.
Existing gates stay green (Makefile is not Python, but the repo's checks must pass). `make lint && make typecheck` both exit 0.
